TLDR: Excellent defensive catcher with current all-fields doubles power and tremendous feel for contact. Other than speed, could end up with all 55s and 60s.
70 runner with feel to hit who is going to play up the middle, possibly at shortstop. Frame gives him a chance to grow into power, too.
He's fine at shortstop so long as you're okay with a non-traditional thowing style. Has remarkable hands in the batter's box. Could benefit from adjustment to lower half.
Do-everything CF without prototypical size.
Athletic, power-hitting college corner outfielder.
Dreaded R/R first baseman without physical projetion or plus-plus raw power but he had a cartoonish sophomore year (.400/.530/.820) and no one doubts he'll hit.
Greene fits the mold of the classic power bat/arm right fielder with a 5 hit tool, like Alex Kirilloff or Trevor Larnach in recent drafts.
Draft eligible sophomore was an athlete to watch in high school, still is somewhat unrefined but has huge tools: 7 speed with power from both sides and fits somewhere up the middle.
Plus power, speed and glove. Bat-to-ball skills are suspect.

Toglia projects for plus raw power and feel to hit from a flat-planed swing that one could argue is well-positioned for the changing big league meta game, in which pitchers are more frequently working up in the zone. He plays mostly first base right now, but is athletic enough to play the outfield long term.
Langliers' is a throwback catcher with a squatty frame, contact over power and above average defensive tools; there isn't much projecting necessary.
Unsigned Braves 2018 first rounder, isnt' done but sounds likely to go to this local JC. Mid-90s, elite curveball spin rate, starter traits, projectable frame.
Bigger-bodied shortstop with power and strikeout issues.
Nastiest two-pitch combo in the draft. Big league ready, but probably just a reliever.
Potential two-way player with huge power projection, natural swing loft and bat-missing curveball.
Projectable, well-rounded shortstop who is young for the class.
Squatty, instinctive corner OF with power, some swing and miss issues.
Very projectable infielder who should grow into above average tools and stay on the dirt.
Lanky middle infielder with flat-planed swing and a sound approach.
Physical prep righty with mid-90s fastball and sound breaking ball.
Hardest-throwing high schooler in the class also has two nasty breaking balls, but you have to reconcile with his atypical mechanics.
Big college corner OF with huge power, big performance and occasionally pitches.
Two-sport athlete with 70 speed despite mature build. Hit tool is a question, as is ultimate physical projection.
Super smooth prep righty with solid average breaking stuff.
College corner OF with high-end peripherals.
